channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Players 1, Umpires 0: Cubs hurler Cody Poteet makes first ABS challenge in spring training and wins
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Chicago Cubs pitcher Cody Poteet believed the 95 mph fastball he threw to Los Angeles Dodgers slugger Max Muncy was in the strike zone. Home plate umpire Tony Randazzo disagreed and called it a ball.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Victor Wembanyama expected to miss remainder of season after blood clot diagnosis, Spurs announce
item.description: San Antonio star Victor Wembanyama's season is likely over after he was diagnosed with a blood clot in his right shoulder, a condition that will sideline one of the league's brightest stars and is the second massive health-related hit for the Spurs in a matter of months.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump calls US hockey team ahead of 4 Nations Face-Off final against Canada
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — President Donald Trump called the U.S. hockey team on Thursday to wish players good luck ahead of their 4 Nations Face-Off final against Canada. USA Hockey posted a photo on social media showing general manager Bill Guerin standing in the middle of the locker room holding up a phone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Walton's family selling items from the late Hall of Fame basketball player's career
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The family of Bill Walton is selling items from the late Hall of Fame basketball player’s career covering his days as a prep player in San Diego to starring at UCLA under coach John Wooden and his time in the NBA along with his broadcasting tenure.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Anthony Davis out at least 2 more weeks, but Mavs say he's making good progress with groin injury
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Anthony Davis will be sidelined at least two more weeks with the groin injury sustained in his Dallas debut, while the Mavericks said Thursday their star forward is making good progress in his recovery.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Spurs star Victor Wembanyama to miss the remainder of the season over blood clot
item.description: San Antonio Spurs star Victor Wembanyama will miss the remainder of the season after being diagnosed with deep vein thrombosis in his right shoulder, the team announced in a statement Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-Spain soccer boss Rubiales found guilty of sexual assault and fined for World Cup kiss
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Former Spanish soccer boss Luis Rubiales was found guilty Thursday of sexually assaulting player Jenni Hermoso for kissing her after the 2023 Women’s World Cup final, an incident that overshadowed Spain’s first world title in women’s soccer and sparked debate about sexism in the sport.
item.dc:creator: TALES AZZONI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Robot umpires are getting their first MLB test during spring training
item.description: A computerized system that calls balls and strikes is being tested during Major League Baseball spring training exhibition games starting Thursday after four years of experiments in the minor leagues.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ex-Spanish soccer president receives fine over unwanted kiss of female player
item.description: Luis Rubiales, the former president of the Spanish soccer federation, was fined over his unwanted kiss of a player at the end of the 2023 Women's World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luka Doncic frustrated by his rough shooting game, but the Lakers believe he'll find his rhythm soon
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Luka Doncic couldn't find his shot in his third game with the Los Angeles Lakers, and LeBron James couldn't make up for it. Yet the Lakers all say they aren't worried after their embarrassing 100-97 loss to the Charlotte Hornets on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former SEAL shared advice with Ohio State football team during Buckeyes' national title run, Ryan Day says
item.description: At the request of Ryan Day, a former Navy SEAL, who also has played in the NFL, gave the Ohio State football team some leadership tips during their College Football Playoff run.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chargers to play 2025 regular season opener in Brazil
item.description: The Eagles and Packers traveled to South America to compete in the first-ever NFL game in South America. The league will return to the continent in September.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USA, Canada hockey stars who fought each other throw subtle jabs ahead of 4 Nations final
item.description: Matthew Tkachuk and Brandon Hagel don't seem to be done sparring with one another, throwing shots ahead of Thursday's 4 Nations final.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With the Dodgers' organization watching, Roki Sasaki has some success throwing live batting practice
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Even in practice, Japanese phenom Roki Sasaki draws a crowd. The 23-year-old threw 27 pitches over two innings of live batting practice on Wednesday in front of hundreds of fans and several teammates.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspects charged in connection to burglaries of several pro athletes
item.description: Seven Chilean nationals face federal charges related to the burglaries of several high profile athletes' homes.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, WGN 9

item.title: Mastroeni prepares Real Salt Lake for a physical battle in Costa Rica
item.description: Real Salt Lake head coach Pablo Mastroeni anticipates a fierce contest as his team travels to Costa Rica for their first competitive match of 2025 — a CONCACAF Champions Cup Round One clash against reigning Costa Rican champions CS Herediano.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: William Byron speaks to NewsNation after Daytona 500 win
item.description: Byron made sure to celebrate his victory, sleeping for an hour before returning to Daytona International Speedway on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Angels star Mike Trout moving from center to right field in hopes of staying more healthy
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — Los Angeles Angels oft-injured star Mike Trout is moving from center to right field in hopes of better preserving his health. The three-time AL MVP played in 82 or fewer games in three of the past four seasons while dealing with an assortment of injuries.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: College student Jaren Barajas beats Damian Lillard in 3-point shooting contest, wins $100K
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Jaren Barajas shoots on his backyard hoop maybe once a month these days. He's a busy 18-year-old college student now, so basketball has taken a backburner to academics.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500 for Hendrick Motorsports
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla (AP) — William Byron fortuitously missed the ferocious wrecks down the stretch at the Daytona 500 that knocked out contenders racing for the checkered flag and left him with a repeat victory in sight.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500
item.description: Ninth in the No. 24 Chevrolet with one lap left, Byron was in the right place by racing near the outside wall in overtime to became the first back-to-back winner since Denny Hamlin in 2019-20.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ludvig Aberg rallies down the stretch at Torrey Pines to win the Genesis Invitational
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Ludvig Aberg never felt worse leaving a golf course than three weeks ago at Torrey Pines when he shared the 36-hole lead only to get violently ill from a stomach ailment and stagger to the finish with a 79.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 7 more massage therapists accuse Justin Tucker of sexually inappropriate conduct: Report
item.description: Seven more massage therapists have accused Baltimore Ravens kicker Justin Tucker of sexually inappropriate conduct, the Baltimore Banner reported Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UCSD student gets the job of a lifetime at Genesis Invitational
item.description: Ruth Nguyen, an 18-year-old student at UCSD, earned the opportunity to announce the names of golf stars at The Genesis Invitational through the TGR Foundation, founded by Tiger Woods, and its Earl Woods Scholarship program.
item.dc:creator: Tony Shin, Fox 5

item.title: CMU Men's Wrestling "Battle in Brownson" recap
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) —Western Colorado Tops Colorado Mesa in ‘Battle in Brownson’ In a heated rivalry matchup inside Brownson Arena, the Colorado Mesa Mavericks faced off against the Western Colorado Mountaineers in the annual Battle in Brownson.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5
